codeReview.summary: FileDropZone component implements all required functionality: drag-and-drop with visual feedback (border highlight on dragover via CSS), file type detection for video/audio/image from extensions, error display for unsupported formats, filename and type badge display after drop, and remove button to reset. However, click-to-browse uses a native file input (<input type="file">) rather than electronAPI.showOpenDialog() as described in the feature description and skill procedure. This is a non-blocking deviation — the native input approach is functionally equivalent and arguably more appropriate for a web-based Electron app, but it creates a documentation inconsistency.

issues.description: handleDrop is marked 'async' but contains no await statements. The async keyword is unnecessary.
handleInputChange is marked 'async' but contains no await statements. The async keyword is unnecessary.
15 instances of '@typescript-eslint/no-explicit-any' warnings in test file. These are in mock implementations and don't affect runtime behavior, but should be properly typed.

Feature description says 'click-to-browse via showOpenDialog', but implementation uses native <input type="file"> element triggered by handleClick. The handoff also mentions 'click handler calling electronAPI.showOpenDialog()'. While functionally equivalent, this deviates from the documented approach.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The frontend-worker SKILL.md for file-drop-zone-component says 'Click handler calling electronAPI.showOpenDialog()', but the actual implementation uses a native <input type="file"> element. The skill procedure doesn't match the actual implementation. This wasn't flagged as a deviation in skillFeedback.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: SKILL.md file section 'file-drop-zone-component' step 1 says 'Click handler calling electronAPI.showOpenDialog()'. Actual code at FileDropZone.tsx:91-93 uses inputRef.current?.click() instead.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The handoff document says 'click handler calling electronAPI.showOpenDialog()' but the code uses native file input. Neither approach is wrong, but there should be consistency between documented procedures and actual implementation.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Handoff file: 'click handler calling electronAPI.showOpenDialog()'. Code: uses <input type="file"> with ref.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The worker discovered and properly implemented the Electron-specific pattern of accessing file.path from dropped files (ElectronFile interface). This pattern is documented in the preload.ts but not explicitly called out in AGENTS.md or the skill. It's important for drag-and-drop in Electron apps because Chromium's dataTransfer.files doesn't include the full path by default.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: FileDropZone.tsx lines 17-19 define ElectronFile interface with path property, and line 43 casts files to ElectronFile[] to access file.path

summary: The FileDropZone component implementation passes review. All core requirements are met: drag-and-drop with visual feedback, file type detection (video/audio/image), error display for unsupported formats, filename/type display, and remove button. The click-to-browse uses a native file input rather than electronAPI.showOpenDialog() as documented — this is functionally fine but creates a documentation inconsistency. No blocking issues found; 245 tests pass, typecheck passes, lint passes (15 warnings), build succeeds.
